text: Just posting a pretty routine outpatient robotic-assisted cholecystectomy.
Nothing really special about this case, honestly.
Favorable patient, body habitus, favorable anatomy.
I'm honestly just trying to get better at editing videos and gaining the confidence to post videos.
So any feedback would be very greatly appreciated.
The surgeon carefully follows the posterior cystic artery branch to ensure it leads into the gallbladder and not back into the liver before ligating it.
